name: lemma_addresses_external_body_roundtrip
reason: `lemma_addresses_in_log_area_subregion_correspond_to_relative_log_positions` is `external_body` — the roundtrip between offset and relative position is trusted without proof; an incorrect modular arithmetic implementation would break the entire log layout

name: relative_pos_wraps_incorrectly
code:
proof fn phi_2_relative_pos_wraps_incorrectly(head_offset: int, log_area_len: int)
    requires
        0 <= head_offset < log_area_len,
        log_area_len > 0,
    ensures
        relative_log_pos_to_log_area_offset(0, head_offset, log_area_len) == head_offset,
        relative_log_pos_to_log_area_offset(log_area_len - head_offset, head_offset, log_area_len) == 0,
{
}
reason: Tests that relative position 0 maps to head_offset and the wrap-around point maps to offset 0 — if the wrapping logic were off-by-one, log reads would be shifted by one byte

name: inverse_not_bijective_at_boundary
code:
proof fn phi_3_inverse_not_bijective_at_boundary(head_offset: int, log_area_len: int)
    requires
        0 < head_offset < log_area_len,
        log_area_len > 1,
    ensures
        log_area_offset_to_relative_log_pos(head_offset, head_offset, log_area_len) == 0,
        log_area_offset_to_relative_log_pos(head_offset - 1, head_offset, log_area_len) == log_area_len - 1,
{
}
reason: The inverse mapping at the boundary (offset just below head) should give the maximum relative position — if the boundary case is wrong, the last byte of the log area would be misaddressed

name: tentative_append_preserves_committed_log
code:
proof fn phi_4_tentative_append_preserves_committed_log(state: AbstractLogState, bytes: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        bytes.len() > 0,
    ensures
        state.tentatively_append(bytes).log == state.log,
        state.tentatively_append(bytes).head == state.head,
        state.tentatively_append(bytes).capacity == state.capacity,
{
}
reason: `tentatively_append` only extends `pending` — if it also modified `log`, `head`, or `capacity`, committed data would be corrupted by tentative operations

name: write_outside_range_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_5_write_outside_range_unchanged(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, addr: int, bytes: Seq<u8>, k: int)
    requires
        0 <= k < view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        !(addr <= k < addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[k] == view.state[k],
{
}
reason: Writing to a range should leave bytes outside that range untouched — if the map-based write implementation leaked into other positions, it would corrupt unrelated log data
